title: oxytropis oxyphylla var. psilocarpa
intro: Oxytropis oxyphylla var. psilocarpa, also known as Psilocarpha oxyphylla, is a plant species in the legume family native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine in the region, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Psilocarpha oxyphylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and boost the immune system.
